findings.title: Raw model keys can be handed through non-canonical env names
findings.description: The model credential guard only checks two exact environment variable names. A construction call such as createModelCredentialSource({ provider: "openai-codex", env: { MODEL_API_KEY: <generated OpenAI-shaped provider key> } }) succeeds and returns host-subscription provenance instead of rejecting. The ticket requires any construction path handed a raw provider key to fail closed.

findings.title: Unknown multi-segment provider tokens pass the redactor and scanner
findings.description: A neutral meta value shaped as provider-prefix, two long numeric dash-separated segments, and a long alphabetic dash-separated segment is neither redacted nor reported by scanSecretLikeText. The gap is in the unknown-token fallback: provider-prefixed multi-segment handling only accepts numeric-only tails or a single opaque suffix, so this secret-shaped token class fails open.

findings.title: Trace JSONL redaction does not cover non-meta LogEvent fields
findings.description: TraceProcessor redacts meta but emits event, sessionId, correlationId, and upid unchanged. If a token-shaped value reaches correlationId or an event suffix, toJsonl emits it raw; the generated secret.redacted event can also echo an unsafe sourceEvent before reports that serialize events() are scanned. SEC-1 says no raw provider key may appear anywhere in JSONL trace artifacts.
